paragraphs.body: The unique ways in which we communicate with one another, whether through personal jokes, phrases, or shared experiences, play a crucial role in building our identities and strengthening connections with others. Inside jokes are an interesting aspect of communication that creates a special bond amongst the groups that you share these inside jokes with. Some look at inside jokes as a negative thing like in the article “No More Inside Jokes” the author Chris Graham says “inside jokes alienate people by making them feel left out, alone, and un-special.” in some cases I think this can be true but to me the term “inside joke” has a whole different meaning. These jokes, phrases, and experiences represent dozens of great memories and a sense of personal belonging. It reminds us that we live in a unique world where everyone should be free to be themselves. These “inside jokes” encourage intimacy and connection, strengthening the ties that bind you together with these certain groups and cultures that others may not understand. The connections and different shared languages made with the people around you are essential, provide strength and create a safe space. Throughout my life I have built these shared languages and connections with special people. Without even realizing it I was slowly creating my own shared language with people.

annotations.comment: I wonder if there's a more intentional and effective intro form you might use to more immediately engage your reader? For example: could you tell a story from your own life about an inside joke?

paragraphs.body: One of the most important shared languages to me is the special connection and bond I have with my sister. This would be referred to as one of my 'home' languages. This language with my sister is through late-night talks and spontaneous road trips. It is all such great fun, and there's so many deep conversations; it feels like a place of belonging, very uniquely our own. Being so close with her we slowly created our own secret phrases and jokes that are only shared between me and her. For example, when my mom takes pictures of us, we jokingly try to sneak in a middle finger in the picture. It may sound immature but to me and my sister it is hilarious, especially when no one else in the photo noticed. We also have inside jokes that only we would understand, like calling our dog "Snoop dogg," after a hilarious moment where he started barking to the beat of a snoop dogg song. Every time my sister says, "I've got you ugly," I know for sure that she has my back, no matter what the situation is. This language of support and understanding gives me security, making me feel at home. I know that I can be my real self without real judgment. This home language carries the most important parts of my identity including my sense of belonging, humor, and loyalty. It is a reflection of myself and the things my sister and I hold dear. This connection speaks to me in a way that gives comfort in a world that oftentimes seems chaotic. It reminds me at every turn that, no matter where life takes me, I will always have my sister standing beside me and speaking this unique language of love and laughter.

annotations.text: One of the most important shared languages to me is the special connection and bond I have with my sister.

It is a reflection of myself and the things my sister and I hold dear. This connection speaks to me in a way that gives comfort in a world that oftentimes seems chaotic.

paragraphs.body: Just like the special bond I have with my sister, I also share a personal and fun language with my best friends that makes our friendship even stronger. We call this “Girl code”. It is a language of friendship. People outside our friend group cannot understand it. This is a secret language, composed of phrases and expressions born from the numerous nightlong conversations, inside jokes, and shared experiences we have. For example, when we say "bing-challing," we are really referencing our group as a whole and the unbreakable bond in our friendships. It is symbolic of loyalty and an understanding that the girls got your back at any moment in your life. Phrases like "burn the bread" literally mean to pass on the latest news or drama, but it also opens a free space where we can just share feelings and experiences without judgment. Another phrase that summarizes our way of empowering one another is "bing always wins." It's not just a slogan, but it is how we uplift one another and celebrate each other's successes. If one achieves something or successfully conquers a problem, we say it to boost morale and hype each other up. During those moments of feeling vulnerable, we say, "Let's break," which really means it is time to sit down and take care of ourselves and each other. It shows that we care about each other and our own mental health. When we are feeling down, the rest come together to say it's okay to lean on each other. This reminds us that outsiders may not understand all of the insight in our communication, but the bonds we share from unique expressions are what matter. In a world that often can feel lonely, "Girl Code" reminds us we are never alone; we have each other. “The languages of connection” To speak a special language is about knowing you can be yourself, have comfort in inside jokes and silly secret phrases and words. To speak a special language is about understanding the importance of laughter and fun. To speak a special language means knowing that you always have people there for you who understand you but also means understanding the value of trust and connection. You must fully appreciate these special shared moments between certain people. Having these special languages means you’re never alone.

paragraphs.body: You're never alone. This is something that is incredibly important to me and was something that was taught to me early on by my soccer coach. "We never walk alone" This was something my soccer coach often said and it was our team's own personal motto. Before every game, we would hold hands and all stand in a line across the sideline and say “we never walk alone” as we stepped onto the field. My coach often repeated this phrase all through my soccer career with that team. I’ve always wondered how my coach came across this quote and the whole idea of it, but it resonated deeply with all of us on the team. After five years with that coach, those words have become a lasting part of my life. Back then, it represented teamwork, a reminder that we always had each other’s backs on the field. We fought for each other, we worked hard together, and we celebrated wins together. It shaped our friendships and taught us about the importance of support and unity. Now this saying carries a deeper meaning for me. It’s a powerful reminder that no matter what challenges we face, we’re never truly alone. Life can sometimes feel overwhelming, filled with personal struggles or moments of doubt, but I find comfort knowing there are friends and family that are there for me and supporting me. This phrase has turned from a simple team motto into something that guides me in life and provides me with comfort. You are never truly alone, there is always someone walking with you.

annotations.text: You're never alone. This is something that is incredibly important to me and was something that was taught to me early on by my soccer coach. "We never walk alone" This was something my soccer coach often said and it was our team's own personal motto.

paragraphs.body: The languages we create with those closest to us are more than just words; they are the threads that weave our identity together. Whether it's through something as silly as secretly trying to sneak a middle finger in a family picture for laughs or just a simple term like “bing always wins” to celebrate the success between friends it all has its own special meanings. Some may see “inside jokes” as a way to disinclude people but I see them as crucial expressions that underline the importance of shared languages and cultures within our circles. I see them as a way to bring importance to these shared languages between certain groups. These connections are necessary, they provide comfort, trust, and a strong sense of unity in a world that can sometimes feel lonely.
